text: This research examines how the intersection of discipline and religious values shapes student resilience and responsibility at SMP Nidaul Quran in Kabupaten Sarolangun. Using a qualitative case study design, data were collected through in-depth interviews, field observations, and an analysis of institutional documents. The findings reveal that discipline serves as a foundational pillar for character development, moving beyond mere rule enforcement to facilitate the internalisation of core virtues. By harmonising behavioral discipline with Islamic spiritual practices, the school successfully fosters students who are both resilient in the face of adversity and accountable for their actions. Key to this process is the role of teachers as moral exemplars and the proactive involvement of parents, creating a robust support system. This study suggests that integrating religious values does more than bolster academic competence; it anchors students with strong moral integrity. The holistic model employed by SMP Nidaul Quran—linking educators, families, and faith—offers a scalable framework for other Islamic educational institutions seeking to cultivate the "hearts and souls" of students, ensuring they are prepared for the complexities of modern life.
